utilityName: City of Chandler Water Quality Backflow Program
dueBasis: Chandler maintains required backflow records through its Water Quality division, routes management through the SAMS cross-connection platform, and requires certified testers approved by the city for accepted testing and maintenance work.
coveredPropertyTypes: Irrigation and outdoor water-use systems where protection is required
Protected services tracked through Chandler Water Quality
officialListLabel: Open the Chandler approved testing companies list
penalties: Chandler treats backflow compliance as an active Water Quality field-services program. The customer risk is staying outside the approved-company workflow or missing the city-managed record path inside SAMS.
sourceExcerpt: Chandler is a strong Arizona utility because the city publishes a live approved testing-company list, tracks assemblies through the SAMS platform, and requires city-approved certified testers.
verdictSummary: Chandler is a strong expansion utility because it combines a city-run Water Quality program, a maintained approved-company list, and explicit city-approved tester requirements.
whoIsAffected: Commercial, multifamily, HOA, irrigation, and other protected Chandler services that Water Quality tracks through its cross-connection workflow.
residentialNotes: Chandler is less broad than Tampa on ordinary homeowner pages, but HOA, multifamily, and outdoor water-use cases can still create real local demand.
Residential and managed-community irrigation issues can still route through the same Water Quality backflow program.
commercialNotes: Chandler is especially strong for commercial and managed-property intent because the city keeps an approved testing-company list rather than leaving tester discovery vague.
The SAMS platform makes this a real operating program, not just a static educational page.

annualTesting.summary: Chandler Water Quality manages the city backflow program and requires city-approved certified testing companies for accepted ongoing testing and maintenance work.
highlights: Water Quality uses the SAMS cross-connection platform for backflow management.
The city publishes an approved testing-company list.
City standards require all backflow assemblies to be tested by a certified tester approved by Chandler.
workflowSteps: Confirm the assembly is in Chandler's active Water Quality program.
Choose a company from Chandler's approved testing list.
Keep the result aligned with the city-managed record workflow.

irrigation.summary: Chandler's backflow program supports real irrigation demand because outdoor water-use systems still route into the same approved-company and city-record workflow.
highlights: Landscape and outdoor water-use systems are part of the same city-managed protection story.
The approved-company list gives irrigation owners a direct next step.
City-approved assembly standards still apply to the installation.
workflowSteps: Confirm the irrigation assembly type and city requirement.
Keep the test and any follow-up work aligned with Water Quality records.

fireLine.summary: Chandler's standards treat fire-protection assemblies as city-approved backflow devices rather than an afterthought.
highlights: City standards call for approved assemblies including fire-protection use when applicable.
Backflow companies in the approved list include fire-focused firms.
Commercial fire-side testing should stay inside the approved-company path.
workflowSteps: Confirm the assembly is serving fire protection.
Use an approved company qualified for the fire-side assembly class.
Keep the accepted test result inside Chandler's managed compliance path.
Confirm the service is tracked by Chandler Water Quality.
Choose a city-approved certified testing company.
Complete the test or maintenance work using the Chandler-approved workflow.
Keep the city-managed record current through the Water Quality program.
failureHighlights: Chandler relies on an approved-company list rather than ad hoc tester selection.
The city uses a real system-of-record platform for backflow management.
Engineering standards reinforce that only city-approved certified testers may perform accepted testing.

costBand.testingRange: Testing is market-priced, but Chandler narrows the market through its approved-company list and managed record system.
costBand.repairRetestRange: Repair and retest cost varies by assembly type and whether the work touches commercial or fire-protection service.
costBand.pricingNotes: The commercial value comes from a live approved-company funnel and strong city program, not from a public flat fee.
